revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:肥後古代の森]]
'''Higo Ancient Forest''' (肥後古代の森) is a historical park in the northern part of Kumamoto Prefecture, encompassing numerous decorated tomb clusters along the Kikuchi River basin, particularly in {{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|Yamaga, Kumamoto|qid=Q861214|lt=Yamaga|1=Yamaga, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Kaō, Kumamoto|en|Kaō, Kumamoto|qid=Q6381942|lt=Kaō|1=Kaō, Kumamoto}}, and {{ill|Kikusui, Kumamoto|en|Kikusui, Kumamoto|qid=Q6406437|lt=Kikushui|1=Kikusui, Kumamoto}}, which have been developed as part of the {{ill|Fudoki Hill|ja|風土記の丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudoki Hill|qq=}} project<ref name="one">{{PDFlink|[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/files/attach/30/30802/22218_1_%E8%82%A5%E5%BE%8C%E5%8F%A4%E4%BB%A3%E3%81%AE%E6%A3%AE.pdf 'Kumamoto Prefecture Cultural Property Development Report 3: Higo Ancient Forest']}} Kumamoto Prefecture Board of Education</ref>.
The Kikuchi River basin, which flows through northern Kumamoto Prefecture, is blessed with a warm climate, fertile soil, and a rich cultural history. The Higo Ancient Forest is a large-scale historical park divided into the {{ill|Yamaga area|ja|山鹿地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga area|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kikushui area|ja|菊水地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikushui area|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Kaō area|ja|鹿央地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaō area|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="two">Archaeological Site Conservation and Local Residents: Japanese Experience and its Application to China 王冬冬[et al.], March 2018</ref>. This basin is one of Japan's most significant regions for decorated tombs, offering a fascinating window into the voices and spiritual world of ancient people through their primitive artwork<ref name="one"/><ref name="thr">Kuwabara, Noriaki. "Historical Focus: Higo Ancient Forest and the Prefectural Decorated Tomb Museum," *Rekishi to Chiri* (493), Yamakawa Publishing, September 1996.</ref>.
== Higo Ancient Forest – Kaō Area ==
The '''Higo Ancient Forest (Kaō Area)''' (14 ha) centers around the {{ill|Kumamoto Prefectural Ancient Burial Mound Museum|en|Kumamoto Prefectural Ancient Burial Mound Museum|qid=Q4557656|lt=Kumamoto Prefectural Ancient Kofun Museum|1=Kumamoto Prefectural Ancient Burial Mound Museum}}, along with the {{ill|Iwabaru Kofun Group|ja|岩原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwabaru Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578830|1=Iwabaru Kofun Group}} and community exchange facilities<ref group="note">Includes a local products center, an ancient plaza, and a lotus garden.</ref>. These decorated tombs not only feature chamber decorations but also integrate skillfully with their surrounding landscape, inviting visitors into a world of natural beauty<ref>Kumamoto Prefecture Gazette. (12284, January 2014)</ref><ref name="thr"/>. Through restoration efforts, the careful planning, refined aesthetic sense, and artistic vision of ancient people become apparent, highlighting the richness of their spiritual world for future generations<ref>Kumamoto Prefecture Gazette. (11847)</ref>.
The park is designed to emphasize the connection between the ancient world and the land of one's homeland, aiming to enhance the appreciation of local heritage. By fostering a sense of pride in one's homeland, the park contributes to the creation of a new regional culture.
=== '''Access:''' ===
- 10 minutes by car from the '''Ueki Interchange''' on the '''Kyushu Expressway''' - 10 minutes by car from the '''Yamaga City Bus Terminal'''
=== '''Project Details:''' ===
* '''Managing Organizations:''' Kumamoto Prefecture Cultural Affairs Division, Yamaga Public Works Office, Kaō Town Hall * '''Design:''' Takashi Hirashima (Planning, Design, Supervision), Kenjirō Sakaida, Kyōji Matsuo, Yukio Yamaguchi (Design), Yasue Hirashima, Hisako Yamamoto (Design & Estimation) * '''Construction:''' Suishōen Co., Ltd., Morita Landscaping Co., Ltd., Serikawa Landscaping, Harada Landscaping, Kumamoto Stone Materials Co., Ltd., Solid Co., Ltd., Toho Perlite Co., Ltd., Iwao Ceramics Co., Ltd. * '''Facilities:''' '''Decorated Tomb Park''', '''Prefectural Decorated Tomb Museum''', '''Kaō Products Center''' * '''Construction Period:''' October 1989 – August 1992<ref>Katō, Shunpei. "Formation and Development of Twin-Ring Designs," Tokai University, March 2017.</ref>.
== Higo Ancient Forest – Kikushui Area ==
The '''Higo Ancient Forest (Kikushui Area)''' (25 ha) is the '''largest''' of the three areas and is located along the '''Kikuchi River''', an important cultural site where people have lived since the '''Jomon period'''. The area has been developed into a '''comprehensive historical park''', allowing visitors to experience the historical background of the decorated tomb clusters and the cultural environment of the river basin<ref>Kumamoto Prefecture Gazette. Heisei 21 (2009) October Issue</ref>.
In addition to historical preservation, the park is designed as a '''cultural hub''' that stimulates the '''local economy''' through facilities such as the '''Higo Folk Village''' and '''Etagawa Riverside Park'''. The park fosters an understanding of '''waterside living culture''' and deepens visitors' connection with nature, expanding the local community’s sense of heritage and environmental awareness<ref>Kumamoto Prefecture Gazette. (12314)</ref><ref name="two"/>.
=== '''Access:''' ===
- 5 minutes by car from the '''Kikushui Interchange''' on the '''Kyushu Expressway''' - 15 minutes by taxi from '''Tamana Station''' on the '''JR Kagoshima Main Line'''
=== '''Project Details:''' ===
* '''Location:''' Ueda, Kikushui, Tamana District, Kumamoto Prefecture * '''Managing Organizations:''' Kumamoto Prefecture Cultural Affairs Division, Tamana Public Works Office, Kikushui Town Hall * '''Design:''' Takashi Hirashima (Planning, Design), Takashi Kitano (Architectural Supervision), Kenjirō Sakaida, Yasue Hirashima, Hisako Yamamoto (Design & Estimation), Hironori Gotō, Tomohiro Nishiyama, Kyōji Matsuo (Landscape Design), Akira Umeda (Architecture), Kenjirō Kyō (Facilities) * '''Construction:''' Kimura Corporation * '''Facilities:''' '''Decorated Tomb Park''', '''Stone Figures Hill''', '''Jomon Village''', '''Higo Folk Village''', '''Etagawa Riverside Park''' * '''Construction Period:''' October 1989 – March 1992<ref>Koratamennaru: Kumamoto Prefectural Information Magazine, July 2001 Issue, Kumamoto Prefecture Public Relations Office</ref>.
== Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kumamoto Prefectural Ancient Burial Mound Museum|en|Kumamoto Prefectural Ancient Burial Mound Museum|qid=Q4557656|lt=Kumamoto Prefectural Ancient Kofun Museum|1=Kumamoto Prefectural Ancient Burial Mound Museum}} * {{ill|Tsukabōzu Kofun|ja|塚坊主古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukabōzu Kofun|1=Tsukabōzu Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18337360}}
